CONTENT.Club career: On 7 July 2011, Alioui signed a one-year stagiaire (trainee) contract with Guingamp. He made his professional debut two weeks later scoring his first professional goal in a 2–0 win over Laval in the Coupe de la Ligue. A week later, he made his league debut appearing as a substitute in a 1–1 draw with Châteauroux.On 5 July 2016, Alioui signed a three-year contract with Ligue 2 side Nîmes. Towards the end of his spell with Nîmes, Alioui suffered two leg injuries which prevented him from playing for more than nine months.On 2 July 2019, Alioui agreed to a three-year contract with Ligue 1 club Angers. On 31 August 2021, he moved on loan to Belgian club Kortrijk.

CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 15 December 2022As of 29 July 2019As of match played 29 July 2019Scores and results list Morocco goal tally first, score column indicates score after each Alioui goal.
CONTENT.International career: Although eligible to play for France, Alioui has appeared for Morocco at various youth levels. He was called up and capped for friendly matches against Gambia U23 and Ivory Coast U23. He made his debut for the senior team in a 1–1 friendly tie with Gabon on 3 March 2014.

SUMMARY: Rachid Alioui (born 18 June 1992) is a professional footballer who plays as a striker for Swift Hesperange in Luxembourg. Born in France, he played for the Morocco national team from 2014 to 2019, scoring two goals in eighteen appearances.
